title: Implement VK ID Auth Module Provider
verify: Provider implements Medusa AbstractAuthModuleProvider under provider ID vkid.
Start creates single-use state and S256 PKCE challenge; callback requires code, state, device_id, and verifier match.
Server-side exchange maps stable VK user_id and required email without persisting provider tokens.
Cancel, malformed payload, network error, expired/replayed state, and PKCE mismatch fail with sanitized errors.

verify.reason: Independent T3 verification found VK token exchange wire incompatibility and missing mismatched device_id proof.
T3 functional and semantic verification passed after bounded retry 1/2.

evidence_required: HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use Authorization Code with S256 PKCE and server-side token exchange.
Use provider user_id as entity ID and require validated email for customer handoff.
Keep VK-specific behavior inside one Medusa Auth Module Provider.
invariants: Provider tokens are never persisted or logged.
State is opaque, short-lived, and single-use.
Raw provider failures and identity data are not returned to storefront.
purpose: Add VK ID as a supported Medusa customer identity provider without creating a parallel auth system.
success_outcome: VK ID start and callback produce a validated Medusa provider identity using state and PKCE while retaining no provider token.
anti_goals: Do not create customer/session or storefront UI in this slice.
Do not store VK access or refresh tokens.
Do not use live VK credentials in automated tests.

stop_conditions: VK API behavior cannot satisfy state and S256 PKCE requirements.
Validated email or stable provider subject cannot be obtained.
Implementation would persist or expose provider tokens.
